devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवमेतन्निगदितं पृष्टवान् यद् भवान् मम । नृणां यन्म्रियमाणानां मनुष्येषु मनीषिणाम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evametannigaditaṃ pṛṣṭavān yad bhavān mama nṛṇāṃ yanmriyamāṇānāṃ manuṣyeṣu manīṣiṇām

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
In this way, I have described to you this course (of study etc. about Hari, as prescribed in the Śāstras)—the course about which your honour asked me for the sake of wise and thoughtful persons among men, especially for those who are about to die.

devanagari: ब्रह्मवर्चसकामस्तु यजेत ब्रह्मणस्पतिम् । इन्द्रं इन्द्रियकामस्तु प्रजाकामः प्रजापतीन् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmavarcasakāmastu yajeta brahmaṇaspatim indraṃ indriyakāmastu prajākāmaḥ prajāpatīn

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: One desirous of the glory of Vedic studies should worship the god Brahmā. He who desires to have power and skill of his sense-organs should worship Indra. He who wishes to have progeny should worship Prajāpatis (like Dakṣa).

devanagari: देवीं मायां तु श्रीकामः तेजस्कामो विभावसुम् । वसुकामो वसून् रुद्रान् वीर्यकामोऽथ वीर्यवान् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: devīṃ māyāṃ tu śrīkāmaḥ tejaskāmo vibhāvasum vasukāmo vasūn rudrān vīryakāmo
translation: He who longs to have prosperity should propitiate the goddess Māyā (Durgā). One who desires to have personal brilliance should worship the fire-god. One wishing for wealth should worship the eight Vasus. A vigorous person desiring for more strength should propitiate the Rudras.

devanagari: अन्नाद्यकामस्तु अदितिं स्वर्गकामोऽदितेः सुतान् । विश्वान् देवान् राज्यकामः साध्यान् संसाधको विशाम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: annādyakāmastu aditiṃ svargakāmo viśvān devān rājyakāmaḥ sādhyān saṃsādhako viśām

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj ]
[vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj ]
[accusative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb]

translation: He who wishes to have ample food and eatables should worship Aditi, while he who desires to attain heaven (svarga), should propitiate the sons of Aditi, viz. the twelve Ādityas. One desiring to possess a kingdom, should worship the Viśvedevas. One aspiring to control his subjects, should worship the Sādhyas.

devanagari: आयुष्कामोऽश्विनौ देवौ पुष्टिकाम इलां यजेत् । प्रतिष्ठाकामः पुरुषो रोदसी लोकमातरौ ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: āyuṣkāmo pratiṣṭhākāmaḥ puruṣo rodasī lokamātarau
translation: One desiring a long life, should propitiate the Aśvin gods (both the Aśvinikumāras). He who desires to have increase in physical strength should worship the earth. A man desiring permanent firmness in his acquired position, should worship the Sky and the Earth, the parents of this world.

devanagari: रूपाभिकामो गन्धर्वान् स्त्रीकामोऽप्सर उर्वशीम् । आधिपत्यकामः सर्वेषां यजेत परमेष्ठिनम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: rūpābhikāmo gandharvān strīkāmo ādhipatyakāmaḥ sarveṣāṃ yajeta parameṣṭhinam

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: One who desires to have beautiful form, should worship the Gandharvas. One desiring to have beautiful women should worship the heavenly damsel Ūrvaśī. One who desires overlordship on all people, should worship god Brahmā (Parameṣṭhin).

devanagari: यज्ञं यजेत् यशस्कामः कोशकामः प्रचेतसम् । विद्याकामस्तु गिरिशं दाम्पत्यार्थ उमां सतीम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: yajñaṃ yajet yaśaskāmaḥ kośakāmaḥ pracetasam vidyākāmastu giriśaṃ dāmpatyārtha umāṃ satīm
translation: One who seeks reputation or success, should worship Yajña, i.e. Viṣṇu. One aspiring after treasures should worship god Varuṇa. One wishing to have learning, should worship god Śiva, and for conjugal love, one should propitiate goddess Pārvatī (chastity incarnate).

devanagari: धर्मार्थ उत्तमश्लोकं तन्तुं तन्वन् पितॄन् यजेत् । रक्षाकामः पुण्यजनान् ओजस्कामो मरुद्‍गणान् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmārtha uttamaślokaṃ tantuṃ tanvan pitṝn yajet rakṣākāmaḥ puṇyajanān ojaskāmo marud‍gaṇān

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ tan class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ tan class 8 verb]

translation: For the sake of righteousness, one should worship Viṣṇu (the god whose glory is excellent). For the continuity of one’s race, one should propitiate the manes (pitṛs). One desiring protection from danger, should worship the Yakṣaṣ. One desiring to have strength, should worship the gods called Maruts.

devanagari: राज्यकामो मनून् देवान् निर्ऋतिं त्वभिचरन् यजेत् । कामकामो यजेत्सोमं अकामः पुरुषं परम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: rājyakāmo manūn devān nirṛtiṃ tvabhicaran yajet kāmakāmo yajetsomaṃ akāmaḥ puruṣaṃ param

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj ]
[vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj ]

translation: One who aspires after kingship or sovereignty, should propitiate Manus, the presiding deities of the eras (manvantaras). He who longs for the destruction of the enemies, should worship Nirṛti (rākṣasas). One desiring all kinds of enjoyments, should worship god Soma. (But) he who desires freedom from attachment or desires, should devote himself unto the Perfect or the Supreme Man.

devanagari: अकामः सर्वकामो वा मोक्षकाम उदारधीः । तीव्रेण भक्तियोगेन यजेत पुरुषं परम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: akāmaḥ sarvakāmo vā mokṣakāma udāradhīḥ tīvreṇa bhaktiyogena yajeta puruṣaṃ param

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: He who wishes to have no desire at all, or aspiring after all kinds of enjoyments or longing to have Liberation due to the exaltedness of his intelligence, should intensely propitiate, by the path of devotion, the Perfect Man beyond limitations.

devanagari: एतावानेव यजतां इह निःश्रेयसोदयः । भगवत्यचलो भावो यद्‍भागवतसङ्गतः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvāneva yajatāṃ iha niḥśreyasodayaḥ bhagavatyacalo bhāvo yad‍bhāgavatasaṅgataḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: The attainment of the highest good is this much, in the case of the worshippers (of Indra etc.) in this world, but it is in the association of the devotees of the Supreme Lord that unswerving devotion unto the Lord is generated (that leads to Final Beatitude).

devanagari: ज्ञानं यदाप्रतिनिवृत्तगुणोर्मिचक्रम् । आत्मप्रसाद उत यत्र गुणेष्वसङ्गः । कैवल्यसम्मतपथस्त्वथ भक्तियोगः । को निर्वृतो हरिकथासु रतिं न कुर...
english_devnagari: jñānaṃ yadāpratinivṛttaguṇormicakram ātmaprasāda uta yatra guṇeṣvasaṅgaḥ kaivalyasammatapathastvatha bhaktiyogaḥ ko nirvṛto harikathāsu ratiṃ na kuryāt
translation: Is there anybody who, having got the blissful satisfaction (of listening to the stories of Hari which is unavailable elsewhere), would not love those episodes, from which arises the knowledge, which completely subsides all whirling waves of passions (like love, hatred etc.), and creates tranquillity of mind and non-attachment to the objects of senses, leading ultimately to the path of devotion which is regarded as the state of liberation itself here and hereafter.

devanagari: शौनक उवाच । इत्यभिव्याहृतं राजा निशम्य भरतर्षभः । किमन्यत् पृष्टवान् भूयो वैयासकिं ऋषिं कविम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: śaunaka uvāca ityabhivyāhṛtaṃ rājā niśamya bharatarṣabhaḥ kimanyat pṛṣṭavān bhūyo vaiyāsakiṃ ṛṣiṃ kavim

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śaunaka said:
After carefully listening to what has been explained to him thus, what other query was again made by the king, the prominent among the Bharata race, to the son of Vyāsa (Śuka) who was a seer of parabrahman and well versed in the Vedic lore?

devanagari: एतद् शुश्रूषतां विद्वन् सूत नोऽर्हसि भाषितुम् । कथा हरिकथोदर्काः सतां स्युः सदसि ध्रुवम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: etad śuśrūṣatāṃ vidvan sūta no kathā harikathodarkāḥ satāṃ syuḥ sadasi dhruvam

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[vocative single from √ su class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ su class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ su class 2 verb]

translation: Oh learned Sūta! You should narrate that to us who are desirous of hearing, for, discourses in the assembly of the devotees of Lord Kṛṣṇa, must definitely lead to the episodes of Hari.

devanagari: स वै भागवतो राजा पाण्डवेयो महारथः । बालक्रीडनकैः क्रीडन् कृष्णक्रीडां य आददे ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai bhāgavato rājā pāṇḍaveyo mahārathaḥ bālakrīḍanakaiḥ krīḍan kṛṣṇakrīḍāṃ ya ādade

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]

translation: Verily, that great warrior king of the Pāṇḍava family, was a great devotee of Kṛṣṇa, as he used to worship Kṛṣṇa as a play, while playing with his toys in the childhood (Or he used to imitate the sports of Kṛṣṇa as a child, in his own childhood).

devanagari: वैयासकिश्च भगवान् वासुदेवपरायणः । उरुगायगुणोदाराः सतां स्युर्हि समागमे ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: vaiyāsakiśca bhagavān vāsudevaparāyaṇaḥ urugāyaguṇodārāḥ satāṃ syurhi samāgame
translation: The venerable son of Vyāsa was an ardent devotee of Vāsudeva. When there is a meeting of the pious devotees of Kṛṣṇa, there must be (the discussion about) the great episodes or excellent virtues of Hari (lit. the god who is greatly praised in the Vedas).

devanagari: आयुर्हरति वै पुंसां उद्यन्नस्तं च यन्नसौ । तस्यर्ते यत्क्षणो नीत उत्तमश्लोकवार्तया ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: āyurharati vai puṃsāṃ udyannastaṃ ca yannasau tasyarte yatkṣaṇo nīta uttamaślokavārtayā

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: This rising and the setting sun verily takes away the life of men, except that period used in discussion etc. about Kṛṣṇa, (the god whose reputation lifts the devotee from darkness or tamas).

devanagari: तरवः किं न जीवन्ति भस्त्राः किं न श्वसन्त्युत । न खादन्ति न मेहन्ति किं ग्रामपशवोऽपरे ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: taravaḥ kiṃ na jīvanti bhastrāḥ kiṃ na śvasantyuta na khādanti na mehanti kiṃ grāmapaśavo

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ jīv class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ jīv class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ jīv class 1 verb]

[nominative dual from √ śvas class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ śvas class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ śvas class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ śvas class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ śvas class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ śvas class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ khād class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ khād class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ khād class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ mih class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ mih class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ mih class 1 verb]

translation: Do not the trees live? Or do not the bellows breathe? Do not the domestic or other beasts eat and have sexual enjoyment?

devanagari: श्वविड्वराहोष्ट्रखरैः संस्तुतः पुरुषः पशुः । न यत्कर्णपथोपेतो जातु नाम गदाग्रजः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: śvaviḍvarāhoṣṭrakharaiḥ saṃstutaḥ puruṣaḥ paśuḥ na yatkarṇapathopeto jātu nāma gadāgrajaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The man, to whose ears the name of Kṛṣṇa (the elder brother of Gada) has not reached, is described as similar to (despicable animals like) dogs, swine, camels and donkeys.

devanagari: बिले बतोरुक्रमविक्रमान् ये । न शृण्वतः कर्णपुटे नरस्य । जिह्वासती दार्दुरिकेव सूत । न चोपगायत्युरुगायगाथाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: bile batorukramavikramān ye na śṛṇvataḥ karṇapuṭe narasya jihvāsatī dārdurikeva sūta na copagāyatyurugāyagāthāḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [accusative plural from √ śru class 5 verb], [ablative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [genitive single from √ śru class 5 verb]
[ablative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [genitive single from √ śru class 5 verb]
[vocative single from √ su class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ su class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ su class 2 verb]

translation: The ears of the man, which do not hear the exploits of Kṛṣṇa, are verily like empty holes. Oh Sūta! the tongue which does not sing of Lord Kṛṣṇa, is evil like that of a frog’s.

devanagari: भारः परं पट्टकिरीटजुष्टं । अप्युत्तमाङ्गं न नमेन् मुकुंदम् । शावौ करौ नो कुरुते सपर्यां । हरेर्लसत्काञ्चनकङ्कणौ वा ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: bhāraḥ paraṃ paṭṭakirīṭajuṣṭaṃ apyuttamāṅgaṃ na namen mukuṃdam śāvau karau no kurute saparyāṃ harerlasatkāñcanakaṅkaṇau vā

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

[nominative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ las class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ las class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ las class 1 verb]

translation: The head (though the best part of the body), even if adorned with turban or a crown, is a great burden, if it does not bow to Mukunda. Or hands, which are adorned with bright gold bangles, are like the hands of a dead body, if they do not worship Hari.

devanagari: बर्हायिते ते नयने नराणां । लिङ्गानि विष्णोर्न निरीक्षतो ये । पादौ नृणां तौ द्रुमजन्मभाजौ । क्षेत्राणि नानुव्रजतो हरेर्यौ ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: barhāyite te nayane narāṇāṃ liṅgāni viṣṇorna nirīkṣato ye pādau nṛṇāṃ tau drumajanmabhājau kṣetrāṇi nānuvrajato hareryau

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [accusative plural from √ vraj class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ vraj class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ vraj class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ vraj class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ vraj class 1 verb]

translation: The eyes of men, which do not carefully see the images of Viṣṇu, are like the eyes (i.e. the big bright spots on the feathers) of peacocks. The feet of men which do not go on pilgrimage to the sacred places of Viṣṇu, are mere vegetations like trees.

devanagari: जीवन् शवो भागवताङ्घ्रिरेणुं । न जातु मर्त्योऽभिलभेत यस्तु । श्रीविष्णुपद्या मनुजस्तुलस्याः । श्वसन् शवो यस्तु न वेद गन्धम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: jīvan śavo bhāgavatāṅghrireṇuṃ na jātu martyo śrīviṣṇupadyā manujastulasyāḥ śvasan śavo yastu na veda gandham

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ jīv class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jīv class 1 verb]
[optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]
[nominative single from √ śvas class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ śvas class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: A mortal, who does not get the dust of the feet of the devotees of the Lord, is as good as a carcass even though alive. The man who has not smelt the fragrance of the Tulasī leaves placed at the feet of Lord Viṣṇu, is only a breathing corpse.

devanagari: तदश्मसारं हृदयं बतेदं । यद्‍गृह्यमाणैर्हरिनामधेयैः । न विक्रियेताथ यदा विकारो । नेत्रे जलं गात्ररुहेषु हर्षः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tadaśmasāraṃ hṛdayaṃ batedaṃ yad‍gṛhyamāṇairharināmadheyaiḥ na vikriyetātha yadā vikāro netre jalaṃ gātraruheṣu harṣaḥ
translation: The heart, which at the time of taking different names of Hari, is not moved, and the change in it is not indicated by tears in the eyes, and bristling of the hair on the body through excessive joy, is verily the hardest granite.

devanagari: अथाभिधेह्यङ्ग मनोऽनुकूलं । प्रभाषसे भागवतप्रधानः । यदाह वैयासकिरात्मविद्या । विशारदो नृपतिं साधु पृष्टः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: athābhidhehyaṅga mano prabhāṣase bhāgavatapradhānaḥ yadāha vaiyāsakirātmavidyā viśārado nṛpatiṃ sādhu pṛṣṭaḥ
translation: Oh beloved Sūta! whatever you speak is dear to our hearts. Please narrate to us what did the great Bhāgavata, the son of Vyāsa, who was well-versed in the knowledge of the Soul and who was well questioned, say to the king?

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां संहितायां । द्वितीयस्कंधे तृतीयोऽध्यायः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmadbhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ dvitīyaskaṃdhe tṛtīyo
